articles.title: HIGGSFIELD LAUNCHES ‘VIBE' EDITOR FOR CREATING MOTION GRAPHICS
articles.summary: Higgsfield's Vibe Motion is a no-code AI tool that enables non-technical users to create professional motion graphics and animations in about 10 minutes for $10 per video. The tool targets small teams and solo founders, allowing them to produce marketing animations, infographics, and dynamic logos that traditionally cost $30,000 and take weeks to develop, positioning the company to become a key marketing tool in the AI-native business workflow. Professional plans start at $17.40 per month.

articles.title: ROBLOX'S 4D CREATION FEATURE IS NOW AVAILABLE IN OPEN BETA
articles.summary: Roblox has launched the open beta of its 4D creation feature, which lets users generate interactive 3D objects that can move and respond to players, moving beyond static models. The feature includes two initial templates: "Car-5" for creating functional cars with spinning wheels and "Body-1" for single-piece objects. Roblox plans to expand the feature by allowing creators to build custom object behaviors and is developing additional AI tools, including "real-time dreaming" for world creation through text prompts.

articles.title: IPHONE 18 PRO MAX'S NEW BATTERY GAINS REVEALED BY LEAKER
articles.summary: Leaks suggest Apple plans to extend its battery-life lead with the iPhone 18 Pro Max, boosting capacity to around 5000 mAh (SIM) or nearly 5200 mAh (eSIM), up from the already class-leading iPhone 17 Pro Max. Combined with the more power-efficient A20 Pro chip and a newer modem, Apple appears set to deliver even longer real-world battery life despite only modest capacity gains.

articles.title: DESIGNING BEYOND THE HAPPY PATH IN DESIGN SYSTEMS
THE RETURN OF THE INTUITIVE DESIGNER IN THE AGE OF AI
articles.summary: Great designers stand out through intuition—tacit, experience-built judgment that helps them navigate ill-defined problems and arrive at elegant solutions—and this human skill is becoming more important as AI automates process-driven, de-skilled parts of design. As tools get better at producing outputs, designers who develop intuition through experience, taste, understanding people and systems, and collaborative play will be best equipped to choose, curate, and imagine genuinely new solutions.

articles.title: DESIGN CAREERS IN THE AGE OF AI: SPECIALIZE OR GENERALIZE?
articles.summary: The rise of AI in digital design is triggering a crisis of professional identity, as the apparent return of the “generalist” risks replacing deep expertise with superficial, AI-generated outputs that homogenize solutions and reduce innovation. The argument is not against AI itself, but warns that real value comes from using AI to deepen learning and cross-disciplinary understanding while preserving strong professional trajectories, rather than outsourcing judgment, thinking, and identity to probabilistic tools.

articles.title: FIGMA WORKFLOW AUTOMATION
articles.summary: Automate DesignOps with an all-in-one suite for auditing systems, renaming variants, and fixing design drift.

articles.title: GRIDDY ICONS
articles.summary: Griddy Icons is a free, open-source icon family with a utilitarian design, featuring 1,100+ icons with 1.5px stroke designed at 24x24px.

articles.title: STITCH VIA MCP
articles.summary: The Stitch Model Context Protocol (MCP) server enables your favorite AI tools, such as Cursor, Antigravity, and the Gemini CLI, to interact directly with your Stitch projects.

articles.title: ERIC BAILEY'S PREDICTIONS FOR THE FUTURE OF ACCESSIBILITY
articles.summary: Organizations will adopt "performative accessibility" solutions that focus on compliance rather than on removing barriers for users, similar to how GDPR led to widespread cookie consent banners rather than better privacy practices. The European Accessibility Act deadline will drive demand for quick-fix accessibility tools, including AI-powered solutions trained on largely inaccessible web code. This approach prioritizes contractual compliance over genuine care for users with disabilities, potentially widening the gap between accessibility work and its real human impact.

articles.title: NEW PHOTOSHOP INNOVATIONS IN 2026 PROVIDE A NON-DESTRUCTIVE WORKFLOW SHIFT
articles.summary: Adobe's 2026 Photoshop update introduces non-destructive adjustment layers called the "Texture Trinity" (Clarity, Dehaze, and Grain) that enable maskable, reversible edits without disrupting the workflow. The update includes Dynamic Text, which allows fluid text manipulation along paths without complex vector workarounds. Firefly-powered AI tools now generate 2K resolution output with improved fidelity and fewer artifacts.

articles.title: WILDISH & CO LIGHTENS UP PACKAGING FOR ETHICAL CHOCOLATE BRAND DIVINE
articles.summary: As cocoa prices rise, Divine Chocolate is leaning into premiumization rather than reducing cocoa, refreshing its packaging with Wildish & Co to better reflect its high cocoa content, Fairtrade roots, and farmer co-ownership. The redesign uses bold, flavour-led colours, illustrative farm scenes, and warmer typography to stand out on shelves, differentiate flavours, and elevate the brand without changing the product itself.

articles.title: THIS DESIGNER JUST MADE PAPER CLIPS ADORABLE WITH A MAGNETIC TABLETOP SHEEP
articles.summary: Designer Xin Se created an adorable magnetic paper clip organizer called "Shearing Magnetic Absorption" that looks like a small sheep, with paper clips attaching to form its fluffy wool.

articles.title: AMAZING PSYCHEDELIC DIGITAL ARTWORKS BY ARGENTINIAN ARTIST JUAN BRUFAL
articles.summary: Juan Carlos Brufal, known as "Mono Azul," fuses influences from famous artists like Picasso, Dalí, Van Gogh, and Warhol, incorporating elements from his background in music and poetry.

articles.title: "YOU CAN'T BE PARALYSED BY PERFECTION": 5 QUESTIONS WITH LAUREN WONG
articles.summary: Lauren Wong, co-founder and creative director of Super Keen, champions action over perfection, consistent brand storytelling that creates meaning, and a hands-on leadership style shaped by both creative rigor and personal change.
